metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4747
metadata.comment:
Rationale: - AP1S1 (AP1-σ1A) and AP1S2 (AP1-σ1B) are the predominant brain expressed AP1 sigma subunits (AP1S3/AP1-σ1C is enriched in tissues other than brain @ Figure S5c).

- AP1G1 (AP1-γ1) is part of AP-1 complexes (PMID:15377783), a strong interactor of AP1-σ1A/B (PMID:9733768) and always found in biochemical fractions matching large protein complexes (PMID:28372831). So when detecting AP1G1 one can assume to be looking at a AP-1 complex.

- high-resolution 4Pi microscopy observed co-localization of AP1G1 and SYT1 in Figure 2 implies that the presynaptic AP1G1 was part of an AP-1 complex together with AP1S1 because the hippocamal neuronal culture used here was AP1S2-deficient.. Experimental description: M&M: "Hippocampal cell culture. Primary hippocampal neurons were isolated from P0-P2 pups of wild type or AP1-σ1B-deficient mice, dissociated by trypsin digestion and plated on Poly-D-lysine-coated glass coverslips."

checks.label: OWL Consistency
